name: Torre del Cejo and Tejada Nueva Loop
name_original: Torre del Cejo y Tejada Nueva - circular desde Aznalcóllar
description: This difficult 41.2-mile mountain bike route from Aznalcóllar explores the Torre del Cejo and Tejada Nueva, offering varied terrain and hist
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aznalcóllar offers diverse terrain, from extensive pastures and traditional Dehesas to mountainous regions and significant natural preserves. The area is characterized by a blend of environments, including the protected Corredor Verde del Guadiamar and the scenic Embalse del Agrio. This varied landscape provides a range of mountain bike trails, from paths through forests and dehesas to more rugged, challenging sections. Best mountain bike trails around Aznalcóllar The most popular mountain bike trail is Aznalcollar a Charcofrío - vuelta, a 25.2 miles (40.6 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete, traversing varied terrain with significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Torre del Cejo and Tejada Nueva Loop, a difficult 41.2 miles (66.4 km) path, known for its scenic routes through forests and dehesas. Local mountain bikers also love the Charcofrío – Viewpoint with Panoramic View loop from Schott Solar CSP, a 52.4 miles (84.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 7 hours 29 minutes. Mountain biking around Aznalcóllar is defined by extensive pastures, mountainous regions, and natural preserves like the Corredor Verde del Guadiamar. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Aznalcóllar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aznalcóllar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Aznalcóllar?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Aznalcóllar, offering a range of experiences from easy to difficult. The area's diverse terrain, including extensive pastures, dehesas, and mountainous regions, provides varied options for riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Aznalcóllar?
A: You can expect a diverse mix of terrain, from paths winding through traditional dehesas and forests to more rugged, mountainous sections. The region features extensive pastures, natural preserves like the Corredor Verde del Guadiamar, and scenic areas around the Embalse del Agrio, ensuring a varied and engaging ride.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Aznalcóllar?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available in Aznalcóllar, perfect for those new to the sport or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Aznalcóllar?
A: Absolutely. Aznalcóllar is well-suited for experienced mountain bikers, with 15 difficult trails available. These routes often feature significant elevation changes and rugged sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ro Carrizal Trail – Camino to Charcofrío loop from Schott Solar CSP&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 65 km with over 900 meters of ascent.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Aznalcóllar?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Aznalcóllar is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable exploration of the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Aznalcóllar?
A: The mountain biking experience in Aznalcóllar is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic routes through forests and dehesas, and the challenging climbs that offer rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Aznalcóllar?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Aznalcóllar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino to Charcofrío – Hanging Planters at El Álamo loop from Schott Solar CSP&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 67 km.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass through significant natural features such as the protected Corredor Verde del Guadiamar and offer views of the Embalse del Agrio. You might also encounter traditional dehesas, which are unique Mediterranean agroforestry systems. For specific viewpoints, consider routes that offer a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3519784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Agrio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, Aznalcóllar has several points of interest. You can find the remains of a Roman aqueduct near the old mine, and the Cerro del Castillo offers panoramic views from the site of a medieval castle. The unique Zawiya, a chapel of Muslim origin, is also located in the local cemetery.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Aznalcóllar?
A: Aznalcóllar, being a town that serves as a starting point for many routes, generally offers various parking options within or on the outskirts of the town. Specific parking facilities for mountain bikers are often available near popular trailheads, though it's always advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs accessible by mountain bike in Aznalcóllar?
A: Yes, the Embalse del Agrio (Agrio Reservoir) is a prominent natural feature in the area, offering scenic routes and opportunities for outdoor recreation. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cota de los Frailes&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible and provide beautiful lakeside views.
tips: A nice long section of trail for a mountain bike or even a gravel bike.

Tejada Nueva is a very small town whose origins probably date back to the 4th century BC.
Apart from a few houses, all that remains are the ruins of an Almohad castle and some baths.
PS10 and PS20 are next to each other. You can see them from far away and look like something from the future. Together they produce about 31 megawatts of electricity.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Torre del Cejo y Tejada Nueva mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult, suitable for experienced mountain bikers. It covers a significant distance of approximately 66 km with over 500 meters of elevation gain, featuring varied terrain that can include undulating paths and short, steep ramps.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of the Aznalcóllar area. You can expect a mix of asphalt and dirt tracks. Some sections may feature pronounced descents and undulating paths with short, steep ramps. After rainfall, parts of the trail, especially those within areas like the Corredor Verde del Guadiamar, can become muddy and wet.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable highlights. You'll pass by the Camino to Charcofrío, the historic Cejo Watchtower, and the intriguing Ruins of the Almohad Castle of Tejada Nueva. You'll also see the modern PS10 Solar Power Plant.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Torre del Cejo y Tejada Nueva trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Aznalcóllar. While specific parking areas for this exact trail are not detailed, you should look for public parking options within Aznalcóllar to begin your ride. As it's a circular route, you'll return to your starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Torre del Cejo y Tejada Nueva mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking trails in natural areas around Aznalcóllar are dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control, preferably on a leash, to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles, including picking up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is during the cooler months, typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these periods, temperatures are more moderate. It is advisable to avoid riding in high summer temperatures due to limited shade on some sections of the trail.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Torre del Cejo y Tejada Nueva circular route or the general natural areas around Aznalcóllar for mountain biking.

faqs.question: What kind of natural scenery and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route traverses a natural environment characterized by Mediterranean vegetation, including cork oaks and holm oaks. You may encounter views of the Agrio reservoir and its surrounding landscapes. The area is part of the Corredor Verde del Guadiamar, which is a wildlife corridor, so opportunities for birdwatching and observing local fauna exist.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will ride along parts of the Cañada Real de Huelva, Camino de Santa Ana, Camino a Charcofrío, Colada de Jureñuelo, and the Corredor Verde, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length (approximately 66 km and over 5 hours), you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. A fully charged phone and a GPS device with the route loaded are also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the Torre del Cejo y Tejada Nueva route?
faqs.answer: While specific named viewpoints are not highlighted, the varied terrain and natural setting, including views of the Agrio reservoir, suggest there will be several scenic spots offering panoramic vistas along the route. Keep an eye out for elevated sections that provide good photo opportunities.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Almohad Castle of Tejada Nueva
